Package com.audiodo.aspen

Class IEqualizerCapability

  • java.lang.Object
    • com.audiodo.aspen.IObservableProperty
      • com.audiodo.aspen.IEqualizerCapability
  • public class IEqualizerCapability
extends IObservableProperty
    The equalizer capability

    This class is an interface to the equalizer capability available on a IDeviceService.
    It can be used to perform actions on the equalizer.

    On session established the capability will start a synchronize process with the remove
    device. During this time most of the API calls will be blocked. When sync is complete the
    observer event get_observable_event_sync_complete will be sent.

      static java.lang.String getObservableEventActivateConfirmation()
      Confirmation from device that the equalizer is activated

      static java.lang.String getObservableEventBypassed()
      Observer event notifying that the Equalizer was bypassed

      Observer will be notified with this event when the Equalizer has been bypassed.
      See is_bypassed for more information.
      static java.lang.String getObservableEventBypassRemoved()
      Observer event notifying that the Equalizer's bypass was removed

      Observer will be notified with this event when the Equalizer's bypass has been removed.
      See is_bypassed for more information.

      Confirmation from device that the equalizer is deactivated

      Observers will be notified with this event when the remote device has confirmed a

      static java.lang.String getObservableEventSaveConfirmation()
      Confirmation from device that the equalizer was saved

      Observers will be notified with this event when the remote device has confirmed a
      save request.
      static java.lang.String getObservableEventSetConfirmation()
      Confirmation from device that the equalizer configuration was set

      Observers will be notified with this event when the remote device has confirmed a
      set or reset request.
      static java.lang.String getObservableEventSyncComplete()
      Notification that the capability is in sync with device

      On session establish the equalizer capability will synchronize with the remote
      device.
      boolean isActivated()
      Check if the equalizer is activated.
      boolean isBypassed()
      Check if the equalizer is bypassed

      Check if the Equalizer processing is bypassed.
      boolean remoteDeviceInSync()
      Check if the capability is in sync with the audio device.
      void reset()
      Reset the configuration of the equalizer.

      This method resets the equalizer configuration to default.
      The effect is the same as calling set with all gains set to zero.
      void save()
      Persistent save of the configuration

      Saves the equalizer configuration on the audio device together
      with the currently set id on the audio device.

      This should be used after set to make the changes persistent.
      void save​(java.lang.String id)
      Persistent save of the configuration for the id

      An asynchronous request to save the configuration and the
      passed in id persistently on the audio device.
      void set​(EqualizerConfiguration configuration)
      Set a configuration on the equalizer.

      An asynchronous request to set a new equalizer configuration (not persistent, @see save).

      • activate

        public void activate()
        Activate the equalizer.

        An asynchronous request to activate the equalizer. Observers will be notified with
        event get_observable_event_activate_confirmation when the change has been
        confirmed by the remote device.

        Note: The equalizer can be controlled independently of the profile but a profile has
        to be active for the equalizer to have any affect, see is_bypassed.

      • deactivate

        public void deactivate()
        Deactivate the equalizer.

        An asynchronous request to deactivate the equalizer. Observers will be notified with
        event get_observable_event_deactivate_confirmation when the change has been
        confirmed by the remote device.

      • set

        public void set​(EqualizerConfiguration configuration)
        Set a configuration on the equalizer.

        An asynchronous request to set a new equalizer configuration (not persistent, @see save). Observers will be notified with event when the change has been confirmed by the remote device.

        It is allowed to call this function before a confirmation has been received on the
        previous request. In this case it's not guaranteed that each call will receive a
        confirmation event. When a confirmation is received, the latest known config will be
        sent, any possible earlier requests will never be sent.

        The equalizer does not need to be activated to set gains.

        Note: To make the changes persistent a call to save is required.

        Parameters:
        configuration - Configuration to set.

      • reset

        public void reset()
        Reset the configuration of the equalizer.

        This method resets the equalizer configuration to default.
        The effect is the same as calling set with all gains set to zero.

      • isActivated

        public boolean isActivated()
        Check if the equalizer is activated.

        Returns:
        True if it is activated, false if it is deactivated.

      • remoteDeviceInSync

        public boolean remoteDeviceInSync()
        Check if the capability is in sync with the audio device.

        Returns:
        True if it is in sync, false if not in sync.

      • save

        public void save()
        Persistent save of the configuration

        Saves the equalizer configuration on the audio device together
        with the currently set id on the audio device.

        This should be used after set to make the changes persistent.

      • save

        public void save​(java.lang.String id)
        Persistent save of the configuration for the id

        An asynchronous request to save the configuration and the
        passed in id persistently on the audio device. The id should be
        fetched from an EqualizerProfile. The request will be sent
        to the audio device if the id or the equalizer configuration differs.

        Observers will be notified with the event get_observable_event_save_confirmation
        when the change has been confirmed by the audio device.

        This should be used after set to make the changes persistent.

        Parameters:
        id - The id of the equalizer configuration

      • getConfiguration

        public EqualizerConfiguration getConfiguration()
        Get the configuration object

        Returns:
        The EqualizerConfiguration object

      • getId

        public java.lang.String getId()
        Get the id set on the equalizer on the audio device

        A synchronous request to get the id. Returns the id on the audio device when it is in
        sync, @see remote_device_in_sync. If not, it will return the last known id.

        Returns:
        The id as a string

      • isBypassed

        public boolean isBypassed()
        Check if the equalizer is bypassed

        Check if the Equalizer processing is bypassed. A bypassed equalizer will not be
        processing audio even if its activated. The Equalizer is bypassed if the Audiodo
        personal sound processing is disabled. The API is still open for use and any changes
        will be saved.

        Returns:
        True if the equalizer is bypassed, false if not bypassed.

      • getObservableEventSyncComplete

        public static java.lang.String getObservableEventSyncComplete()
        Notification that the capability is in sync with device

        On session establish the equalizer capability will synchronize with the remote
        device. On completion the observers will be notified with this event. The subject
        parameter is not used for this event.

        Returns:
        A string that identifies the event.

      • getObservableEventActivateConfirmation

        public static java.lang.String getObservableEventActivateConfirmation()
        Confirmation from device that the equalizer is activated

        Observers will be notified with this event when the remote device has confirmed a
        activate request. The subject parameter is not used for this event.

        Returns:
        A string that identifies the event.

      • getObservableEventDeactivateConfirmation

        public static java.lang.String getObservableEventDeactivateConfirmation()
        Confirmation from device that the equalizer is deactivated

        Observers will be notified with this event when the remote device has confirmed a
        deactivate request. The subject parameter is not used for this event.

        Returns:
        A string that identifies the event.

      • getObservableEventSaveConfirmation

        public static java.lang.String getObservableEventSaveConfirmation()
        Confirmation from device that the equalizer was saved

        Observers will be notified with this event when the remote device has confirmed a
        save request. The subject parameter is not used for this event.

        Returns:
        A string that identifies the event.

      • getObservableEventSetConfirmation

        public static java.lang.String getObservableEventSetConfirmation()
        Confirmation from device that the equalizer configuration was set

        Observers will be notified with this event when the remote device has confirmed a
        set or reset request. The subject parameter is not used for this event.

        Note: If multiple set or reset requests are done before a confirmation all requests
        might not receive a confirmation. Only the latest known value will be sent and
        confirmed in this case.

        Returns:
        A string that identifies the event.

      • getObservableEventBypassed

        public static java.lang.String getObservableEventBypassed()
        Observer event notifying that the Equalizer was bypassed

        Observer will be notified with this event when the Equalizer has been bypassed.
        See is_bypassed for more information.

        Returns:
        A string that identifies the event.

      • getObservableEventBypassRemoved

        public static java.lang.String getObservableEventBypassRemoved()
        Observer event notifying that the Equalizer's bypass was removed

        Observer will be notified with this event when the Equalizer's bypass has been removed.
        See is_bypassed for more information.

        Returns:
        A string that identifies the event.
